name: Bobalky
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine butter, sugar, and salt.
2. Add scalded milk; let mixture cool until lukewarm.
3. Dissolve yeast in water.
4. Gradually add the dissolved yeast to the cooled mixture.
5. Incorporate enough flour to form a soft, elastic dough.
6. Turn dough onto a floured board and knead.
7. Place dough in a greased bowl and let rise until doubled in size (1 to 1½ hours) in a warm place, free from drafts.
8. Punch down the dough with fingertips.
9. Let dough rise again for about 45 minutes.
10. Toss lightly on a floured board, knead, and shape into a roll (about ½ inch in diameter).
11. Place rolls on a cookie sheet.
12. Cut with the edge of a teaspoon into small pieces.
13. Let rise for 10 minutes.
14. Bake at 375°F for 15 minutes or until lightly browned.
15. When cool, break and place in a colander.
16. Pour boiling water over bobalky.
17. Place on serving dish, mix with ground poppy seeds (1/2 cup) cooked in 3/4 cup water for 10 minutes, and melted butter, and serve.
Poppy seed mixture: Cook ground poppy seeds (1/2 cup) in 3/4 cup water for 10 minutes. Boil 3 cups milk and 1 cup sugar, then mix with the poppy seed mixture. Alternatively, honey may be used instead of sugar, poured over the bobalky. If your family is small, divide the dough into two portions; bake bobalky from one portion and a loaf of bread from the other.
